title: In Romania, Hard-Hit by Covid, Doctors Fight Vaccine Refusal
content: An anti-vaccine clarion call by leading religious figures, echoed by prominent politicians and social media, helps explain why Romania now has the world’s highest Covid death rate.
en_title: In Romania, Hard-Hit by Covid, Doctors Fight Vaccine Refusal
en_content: An anti-vaccine clarion call by leading religious figures, echoed by prominent politicians and social media, helps explain why Romania now has the world’s highest Covid death rate.

title: When the Taliban Are in Your Bedroom
content: As armed Taliban inspected the New York Times bureau in Kabul, they were escorted by a journalist who used to be a U.S. Marine. The photo of him in uniform was plain for all to see, and ponder.

des_facet: United States Defense and Military Forces

en_title: When the Taliban Are in Your Bedroom
en_content: As armed Taliban inspected the New York Times bureau in Kabul, they were escorted by a journalist who used to be a U.S. Marine. The photo of him in uniform was plain for all to see, and ponder.

title: Brain Drain From Britain Delivers Financial Boon to Estonia
content: The former Soviet satellite state is welcoming British companies looking to escape the tangle of regulations and financial obstacles of doing business in Europe.
en_title: Brain Drain From Britain Delivers Financial Boon to Estonia
en_content: The former Soviet satellite state is welcoming British companies looking to escape the tangle of regulations and financial obstacles of doing business in Europe.

title: Palestinians Were Targeted by Israeli Firm’s Spyware, Experts Say
content: The findings of a new analysis raised questions about whether the Israeli government may have been behind the hacking, using spyware from the NSO Group, a surveillance company blacklisted by the United States.

more_info.byline: By Patrick Kingsley and Ronen Bergman
des_facet: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

en_title: Palestinians Were Targeted by Israeli Firm’s Spyware, Experts Say
en_content: The findings of a new analysis raised questions about whether the Israeli government may have been behind the hacking, using spyware from the NSO Group, a surveillance company blacklisted by the United States.

title: Brazil’s Far-Right Disinformation Pushers Find a Safe Space on Telegram
content: In a social media ecosystem facing mounting pressure to combat fake news and polarization, Telegram is the most permissive disseminator of content — and disinformation.

more_info.byline: By Ernesto Londoño, Flávia Milhorance and Jack Nicas

en_title: Brazil’s Far-Right Disinformation Pushers Find a Safe Space on Telegram
en_content: In a social media ecosystem facing mounting pressure to combat fake news and polarization, Telegram is the most permissive disseminator of content — and disinformation.

title: Oscar Pistorius, Olympic Runner Convicted of Murder, Is Up for Parole
content: The double-amputee sprinter has served half his sentence for killing his girlfriend. His victim’s parents were said to be “shocked” at the prospect of his release.
en_title: Oscar Pistorius, Olympic Runner Convicted of Murder, Is Up for Parole
en_content: The double-amputee sprinter has served half his sentence for killing his girlfriend. His victim’s parents were said to be “shocked” at the prospect of his release.

title: U.S. Travel Ban Ends, Easing Personal Frustrations and Diplomatic Tension
content: Europeans were kept apart from family and friends as the ban dragged on long after E.U. leaders had opened their borders.
en_title: U.S. Travel Ban Ends, Easing Personal Frustrations and Diplomatic Tension
en_content: Europeans were kept apart from family and friends as the ban dragged on long after E.U. leaders had opened their borders.

title: French Catholic Church Will Sell Assets to Compensate Abuse Victims
content: The measure was one of several measures announced by French bishops, one month after a landmark report on sexual abuse by clergy members in France.
en_title: French Catholic Church Will Sell Assets to Compensate Abuse Victims
en_content: The measure was one of several measures announced by French bishops, one month after a landmark report on sexual abuse by clergy members in France.

title: To Steer China’s Future, Xi Is Rewriting Its Past
content: A new official summation of Communist Party history is likely to exalt Xi Jinping as a peer of Mao and Deng, fortifying his claim to a new phase in power.
en_title: To Steer China’s Future, Xi Is Rewriting Its Past
en_content: A new official summation of Communist Party history is likely to exalt Xi Jinping as a peer of Mao and Deng, fortifying his claim to a new phase in power.

title: Nicaragua Descends Into Autocratic Rule as Ortega Crushes Dissent
content: President Daniel Ortega quashed the opposition and struck fear in voters, all but guaranteeing his victory in a presidential contest on Sunday.

title: Fears of Wider Instability in Iraq After Attack on Prime Minister’s Home
content: Armed drones struck the Iraqi prime minister’s home in what was seen as a warning as Iranian-backed groups dispute the results of parliamentary elections.
en_title: Fears of Wider Instability in Iraq After Attack on Prime Minister’s Home
en_content: Armed drones struck the Iraqi prime minister’s home in what was seen as a warning as Iranian-backed groups dispute the results of parliamentary elections.

title: International travelers eagerly await reunions with their loved ones as the U.S. travel changes near.
content: On Monday, travelers from many other countries who are fully vaccinated against the coronavirus will be allowed to enter the United States.
en_title: International travelers eagerly await reunions with their loved ones as the U.S. travel changes near.
en_content: On Monday, travelers from many other countries who are fully vaccinated against the coronavirus will be allowed to enter the United States.

title: Police Officer Stabbed on French Riviera, Authorities Say
content: Gérald Darmanin, France’s interior minister, said an assailant had stabbed an officer in Cannes. The motive for the attack was not immediately clear.

title: Afghan Military Pilots, on the Run, Feel Abandoned by U.S.

title: Costly tests may deter Canadians from making quick trips to the U.S.
content: Canada requires travelers to present the results of an expensive Covid test to enter, deterring short hops across the border.

title: Thousands of Canadians head for the southern United States again.
content: With the border opening to tourists, thousands of Canadian “snowbirds” are on their way to Florida, Arizona and California with campers in tow.
en_title: Thousands of Canadians head for the southern United States again.
en_content: With the border opening to tourists, thousands of Canadian “snowbirds” are on their way to Florida, Arizona and California with campers in tow.

title: Northern Ireland’s health minister is suing Van Morrison over Covid-19 criticism.
content: The Belfast-born singer-songwriter has embraced conspiracy theories and criticized restrictions to slow the spread of the coronavirus. Last year, he released a single called “No More Lockdown.”

en_content: The Belfast-born singer-songwriter has embraced conspiracy theories and criticized restrictions to slow the spread of the coronavirus. Last year, he released a single called “No More Lockdown.”

title: ‘It had just been too long’: Vaccinated international travelers enter the U.S. as restrictions lift.

content: The lifting of rules comes as the country records some of its highest daily case numbers since the pandemic began.

title: Inside a Volcanic Ritual on the Indonesian Island of Java

title: What’s in a Name? For the Koreans of Sakhalin, an Anguished History
content: Stranded for decades on the island in Russia’s Far East, some bear three names, Japanese, Russian and Korean, representing different historical chapters. Now, many are planning their return.

title: Veteran U.S. Diplomat Comes Under Criticism for a Trip to Myanmar
content: Bill Richardson, a former ambassador to the U.N., said that he had held “productive” talks with the general who led the February coup. Rights activists said he gave the junta an air of legitimacy.
